exemplar.title:
To what extent has One Acre Fund improved the quality of life of the people
of Kiboswa village in Kisumu county?

detailedExemplarData.title:
To what extent has One Acre Fund improved the quality of life of the people
of Kiboswa village in Kisumu county?

subject.title: Geography
subject.description:
**IB Geography** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **physical and human geography, environmental interactions, global challenges, and spatial patterns**, with a strong focus on **fieldwork, data analysis, case studies, and Geographic Information Systems (GIS)**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Geographic themes - structured questions | 2h 15min | 2h 15min | SL: 35% / HL: 35% |
| **Paper 2** | Geographic perspectives - data response and essays | 1h 15min | 1h 15min | SL: 40% / HL: 45% |
| **Paper 3** | Global interactions - extended essay responses | N/A | 1h | HL: 20% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Fieldwork investigation (2,500 words) | 20 hours | 20 hours | SL: 25% / HL: 20% |

strengths: Topic and purpose are clearly stated with defined geographic scope and variables
Research question is focused on One Acre Fund and Kiboswa village
Table 1 effectively links quality-of-life indicators to methodology
weaknesses: Key concept âquality of lifeâ remains only partially defined
Verbose and vague phrasing (e.g., âcomposite perspectiveâ) reduces clarity
Methodology lacks detail on sampling frame, instrument design and ethical considerations
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work sits solidly in the 3â4 band. The topic is communicated adequately with sufficient clarity and focus, and the research question is appropriately narrow. Methodological description demonstrates a basic range of sources and methods but omits critical details on design and ethics. Overall the criterion merits 4/6.

strengths: Shows clear understanding of microfinance principles and links to sustainability and food security
Relevant geographic terminology is mostly accurate
Provides a succinct geographic context for Kiboswa village
weaknesses: Explanations are often superficial or assertive rather than analytical
Key geographic theories (e.g., sustainable livelihoods) are hinted at but not integrated
Occasional misuse of terms and inconsistent capitalization reduce precision
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission demonstrates adequate subject knowledge with partially effective use of sources and mostly accurate terminology, fitting the 3â4 band but not rising to excellence. A score of 3/6 reflects clear but limited depth and theory integration.

strengths: Primary and secondary data are applied with clear relevance to the research question
Identification of trends and use of graphs shows purposeful analysis
Argument follows a logical structure and demonstrates methodological triangulation
weaknesses: Statistical depth is minimal (no significance testing or detailed summary statistics)
Several conclusions are asserted without adequate evidence or critical evaluation
Heavy reliance on One Acre Fundâs own materials limits objectivity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Across research application, analysis and argument strands the work falls in the lower half of the 4â6 band. Relevance is strong but depth of evaluation and critical balance is limited. A holistic score of 4/12 reflects adequate critical thinking with room for more rigorous analysis.

strengths: Follows the required structure (title page, contents, sections, references) supporting reader comprehension
Sub-headings and numbered sections align with the research process
Tables and figures are embedded and labelled correctly
weaknesses: Inconsistent font sizes, mis-aligned tables and low-resolution images detract from professionalism
Indentation and formatting hierarchy are not standardized
Layout conventions are only partially applied despite correct pagination and labeling
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Structure is clearly appropriate (band 3â4), but layout shows inconsistencies and visual issues placing it in the 1â2 band for presentation. A combined holistic score of 3/4 adequately captures these strengths and weaknesses.

strandSelections.reasoning: Primary data, secondary reports and maps are employed throughout and clearly relate to the research question; the separation of member/non-member/ex-member datasets demonstrates purposeful application.  Relevance is strong, but occasional over-reliance on One Acre Fundâs own publicity weakens objectivity, so the strand fits comfortably in the 4-6 band, mid-high level.

The student describes primary (questionnaires/interviews) and secondary methods and justifies sampling (members, non-members, ex-members, officials). Basic tables and graphs are produced.  Nonetheless the methodology lacks detail on questionnaire design, sampling frame, statistical treatment and validity/ethical considerations.  Source range is limited (mainly organisationâs own material and a few web pages).  These limitations align with a low-to-mid 3-4 band score.

The essay shows an understanding of microfinance principles, agricultural inputs, gender and yield concepts and links them to syllabus ideas such as sustainability and food security.  However, explanations are often superficial or assertive (e.g., âOne Acre Fund is the solution to food insecurityâ), and key geographical theories (e.g., sustainable livelihoods, development indicators) are only hinted at.  Sources are used but mainly descriptive.  This is consistent with adequate but not extensive subject knowledge.

Pagination, word count and title are present; tables and charts are embedded and labelled.  Nevertheless, inconsistent font sizes, mis-aligned tables and several low-resolution images detract from professional appearance.  Layout conventions are met only âpartiallyâ, aligning with mid 1-2 band, but because two points (tables and figures labelled) are done reasonably, a high 1-2 band mark of 3 is justified.

Terms such as âmicro-creditâ, âagro-forestryâ, âconvectional rainfallâ, âintensive/extensive farmingâ are employed and mostly accurate.  Occasional misuse (âgrevilled treesâ, âdemographic dividendâ used loosely) and inconsistent capitalisation reduce precision.  Overall usage is mostly correct but not consistently so, matching the 3-4 band at 3 marks.

The topic â the social, economic and environmental impacts of micro-finance through One Acre Fund in a single Kenyan village â is explicitly stated in both the title and the opening pages.  The geographic area, population and key variables are all introduced, giving the reader a workable sense of purpose.  However, some wording is verbose (âcomposite perspectiveâ, âquality of life in both social and economic, education and empowerment dimensionsâ) and the boundaries of the concept âquality of lifeâ remain only partially defined.  Overall clarity is adequate rather than exemplary, fitting the middle band at its top end.

The narrative follows a logical introductionâmethodsâanalysisâconclusion pattern and constructs an argument that One Acre Fund improves living standards.  While generally coherent, the argument sometimes drifts into repetition and contains unsupported generalisations (âpeople moved to the global middle classâ).  Minor inconsistencies and limited counter-argument keep it at a modest 4 within the 4-6 band.

The student compares yields, land tenure and gender data and produces graphs.  Trends are identified and linked to quality-of-life claims, yet statistical depth is minimal (no averages for non-member set, no significance testing) and several conclusions are asserted rather than demonstrated.  Evaluation of limitations is brief.  This represents adequate-to-good analysis, best placed at the lower end of the 4-6 band.

The essay follows the required EE structure with title page, contents, numbered sections, references and appendices, aiding reader comprehension.  Sub-headings closely reflect the research process.  Although some sections are overly long and figures occasionally disrupt flow, the overall structure clearly supports understanding, warranting the top band score.

The research question is presented prominently and is focused on a specific organisation (One Acre Fund) and locality (Kiboswa village).  It is sufficiently narrow to be answered within 4 000 words, but the phrase âto what extentâ¦improved the quality of lifeâ still bundles multiple indicators without specifying measurable criteria, so the focus is only partial.  This places the work solidly in the 3-4 band.

annotations.comment: The geographic description omits map scale and coordinate references; include precise spatial metrics or scale bars for geographic rigor.
The justification for site selection is verbose and circular; streamline language to clearly articulate why Kiboswa offers optimal data accessibility.
Figure 1 is well-labelled with a clear source citation, effectively supporting the geographic context.
Claims of yield doubling lack statistical evidence; include summary statistics or significance tests to support assertions.
Percentage figures (e.g., 73.3%) appear without explanation of calculation; outline how percentages were derived from raw data.
The geographic context section succinctly situates Kiboswa village, providing essential locational details and environmental characteristics.
Hypothesis 2 assumes a causal impact of One Acre Fund on environmental knowledge; clarify how this will be measured (e.g., survey scales, interview metrics).
This aims section is verbose and could be broken into numbered bullet points to improve clarity and reader navigation.
The title clearly identifies the subject and establishes the focus on geography, engaging the reader effectively.
The topic statement uses vague phrasing like âcompositesâ without defining how social, economic, and environmental impacts will be measured; refine definitions to sharpen focus.
The research question bundles multiple indicators but does not specify measurable criteria; consider articulating specific variables (e.g., income, food security index) to enhance focus.
The table of contents is comprehensive and clearly guides the reader through each section, reflecting a logical structure.
Subsection indentation is inconsistent, making it harder to distinguish hierarchy; standardize formatting for improved readability.
Incorporate dot leaders (e.g., â1. INTRODUCTION ........ 3â) to visually connect section titles and page numbers.
The introduction header frames the section effectively, but the concept âquality of lifeâ remains undefined; provide a concise operational definition.
The description of One Acre Fund relies heavily on quotations and lacks critical analysis; integrate more evaluation of source credibility and relevance.
While microfinance is defined, the link to geographical theories (e.g., sustainable livelihoods framework) is not explicit; integrate theory to deepen understanding.
Figure 2 appears pixelated and low-resolution, detracting from professional presentation; use higher-quality imagery or vector maps.
The captions for both maps are nearly identical; consider differentiating descriptions to highlight unique perspectives of each figure.
Hypotheses are clearly stated but numbering is inconsistent with section formatting; ensure uniform numbering and styling to maintain methodological clarity.
Table 1 effectively synthesizes quality-of-life indicators and relevant factors, demonstrating a clear methodological link.
The syllabus link is described generically; cite specific syllabus topic numbers or key terms to demonstrate explicit alignment.
Detail the sampling frame and ethical approval process for primary data collection to strengthen methodological transparency.
The integration of primary and secondary data sources shows thoughtful methodological triangulation, enriching research depth.
Expanding on what âcomposite perspectiveâ entails is necessary; define each data dimension explicitly to avoid ambiguity.
The sample size of n=15 for members is given but lacks justification; include a rationale or margin of error estimation for sample adequacy.
Although â2.2 Raw dataâ is clearly labeled, consider subdividing by dataset (members, non-members) for even clearer presentation.
